## [00:00 - 03:00]

Hey, this is D-Patch and I want to take you through a tool called Hotjar that I use for insights when it comes to understanding why people are doing what they're doing on the website. So if you have analytics installed, analytics will tell you what they're doing, but it may not always tell you why they're doing it. So I prefer to use Hotjar because it's a tool I've used for many years. There are other tools out there. Lucky Orange is another pretty decent tool and there are probably others out there as well. Essentially the reason to use a tool like this, number one, I'm looking for heat maps to see what people are doing on the website. I'm recording their sessions anonymously so I can see what people are doing on the website itself, on the page and kind of progressing through different pages. I'm looking at funnels so I can build simple funnels out to see how people are progressing through my site and then I'm running polls which are mini surveys that appear whilst they're browsing so I can get immediate feedback on potentially any blockers, what was working, what's not as well. This particular install of Hotjar that I'm going to take you through is literally fresh off the press when it comes to launching this course. So I'm going to take you through my course launch website. The data's only been there for four days so there isn't a ton of data. I want to take you through the concepts and give you an idea of how you can use this on your website as well. So I'm in there to site dashboard for Hotjar. I'm going to click into heat maps first of all just to show you what I've got going on. When you start off with heat maps you click new heat map, give the URL of the page that you want to track and then Hotjar will start to collect data. So you can see over the last four days that this has been live I've collected 284 page views and under three page views of each of these pages. I will say that I'm on the I think the lowest tier of Hotjar so the data collected isn't 100% and that uses some sampling and I've used this just because I wanted to obviously see some insights but I wanted to test this and set this up for training as well. When the forward traffic goes into the course I'll be upgrading so I can get more data and more insights as well but this is just really set up for deadline purposes. So now if I look at my main page heat map if I click here there's a few things that I can look at. So I can look at where people are clicking. I can see where people are roughly scrolling and where can the mouse moves are going in from as well. So here by default it's selected clicks and I can see mobile versus desktop as well. And what I can see here is where people are roughly clicking and what's quite interesting

sometimes is people will click on things that should not be clicked and so that kind of gives you an idea of whether something maybe is presented incorrectly not in the best way. So looking at click data gives you an idea of where people are kind of clicking some of them just random maybe they're just going to click into scroll down and move the page and as you can see here if I can go down there's different clicks going on let's just find my call to action this is a really really long sales page let's scroll the way down to the bottom where's my call to action. So interestingly here like there's a breakdown of what people are getting and it may be that people were actually clicking here to actually see if there's more information and actually based on this I did add some more information into the live site as well so just by thinking about why people are clicking the fact that people are clicking on literally every single element made me think that people are looking for more information and that's why I wrote out some additional information as well but then you kind of look here there's a promotion code here people were obviously clicking to copy and paste some people are clicking here on the price point you can see that the numbers are start out this is just a privacy thing in hot jar and then people are clicking here to join now I can see that some clicks were also going on here and some people are clicking down here in the FAQs which I'm not entirely sure what's going on but I can see here some clicks here and it's my privacy policy so just gives you an idea of where people are clicking as well and then you can kind of go into the mobile view the same kind of data all the way through obviously here it takes into account the the taps on the mobile screen and it collects that data and overlays it on your screen as well now if I go back to desktop just to illustrate I can also look at how far people are scrolling as well so that loads up it'll give me a kind of temperature gauge of where most of the scrolling happens so 100% of people reach this point well that's because it's above the fob so 100% of people will obviously be there on average most people got to here and then exit it so my bounce rate was not great so just as a fyi for you guys that are watching this as a training video the original plan for this was to launch a video introducing the course and didn't have time after setting this up initially a video would have performed far better because this kind of long sales format

## [15:15 - 16:31]

I think I launched the course on um based on just getting a discounted price out for people who were on early early on and what I wanted to do was to build up the kind of features and developments as I kind of went on and then went into kind of a full-price launch what I didn't have time for was to launch a payment plan and that was the number one reason via the survey you can see there's only one response here but also based on feedback from people via email that people didn't sign up so a good number of this 90% we're looking for a payment plan and so again that's where this kind of data really comes into use you can install this on your product page on your cart page on your checkout page any key page that you want some key insights into to try and figure out the why behind the what and so kind of combining all these tools together place such a big part in improving your conversion rate and I absolutely recommend whether it's hot jar or another tool that you do use a tool like this to give you buys on exactly what's going on on your website you
